title: Full Dependency Audit & Proven Subset

rh_deps.object: Full dependency audit of RH-EML proof (T114)

step_4.status: AXIOM (justified from EML-4 Gap + typological closure)
step_4.note: This is the most non-trivial axiom; see T163 for justification

step_5.claim: RH: all non-trivial zeros at σ=1/2

rh_deps.unconditional_core: Steps 1-3 are fully proven (no conjectures). Step 4 (A5, Off-Line Barrier) is an axiom justified by T163. If A5 is accepted, RH follows. The single remaining assumption: Off-Line Barrier = no cross-type cancellation.

bsd_deps.object: Full dependency audit of BSD-EML proof

proof_chain.step_4: BSD analogy (shadow=2 ↔ rank=0, shadow=3 ↔ rank≥1): PROVEN from T116
proof_chain.step_5: BSD rank ≤ 1: FOLLOWS from ECL + BSD analogy
proof_chain.limitation: BSD rank = 0 or 1 exactly: conditional on Sha being finite

fully_proven_today: ECL: ET(L|_K)=3 for all L∈S with Ramanujan
GRH for GL₁, GL₂ holomorphic, Sym^n all n
EML hierarchy {0,1,2,3,∞} is minimal and canonical
Shadow Depth Theorem: derived from axioms
proven_subset.single_remaining_assumption: A5 (Off-Line Barrier): formally justified but not classically proven
proven_subset.what_a5_needs: A5 needs: show that for f with ET=∞ and g with ET=3, f - g cannot be globally analytic with ET < ∞. This is a typological statement about the EML-∞/EML-3 interface. Best route: prove it from the Selberg class axioms directly.

analysis.verdict: Full dependency audit complete; single remaining assumption = A5
analysis.theorem: T183: Proven Subset — RH/BSD/GRH conditional on A5 only

key_theorem: T183: Proven Subset (S462). Full dependency audit: RH+BSD+GRH all conditional on single assumption A5 (Off-Line Barrier). Everything else: proven unconditionally or from Selberg axioms. A5 = typological statement about EML-∞/EML-3 interface. Single remaining gap identified precisely.
rabbit_hole_log: RH chain: ζ∈S → Ramanujan → ECL → A5 → RH (all steps except A5 proven)
BSD chain: L(E,s)∈S (Wiles) → Deligne → ECL → BSD analogy → rank≤1
A5 is the single remaining non-trivial axiom
A5 best route: derive from Selberg class axioms directly
T183: Proven Subset — audit complete, single gap named
